summary:
The term “apical stop” refers to a specific technique where material such as gutta-percha is placed at the tip of the root canal during endodontic treatment to halt further movement into the apical region. This prevents overextension of filling material past the apex by creating physical obstruction using instruments designed for this purpose—such as those with tapered tips—which ensures proper seal formation without compromising tissue integrity around the tooth’s base.

The apical stop is a procedural step involving placement of dental material at the root apex to physically block excessive extension of the obturation material, ensuring effective sealing while preserving peri-apical tissues from damage due to improper fillings. It serves both functional and protective roles within endodontics through precise control of instrument positioning near the apex.
